title: client::info() declares 14 individual mut variables before struct init
description: client.rs Client::info() declares 14 separate mut local variables (version, build, comm_data_len, ..., nvm_state) purely to pass as out-pointers to wh_Client_CommInfo, then immediately moves them into a ServerInfo struct. The pattern is verbose and invites mismatches between variable names and struct field names. Suggested rewrite: zero-initialise a ServerInfo via Default (or MaybeUninit), then pass pointers to its fields directly. If the C API makes that awkward, at minimum declare all 14 as a single block comment-separated group rather than one-per-line to reduce the visual noise.
